human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for electronics-retail boundary discipline, source quality, order and payment evidence handling, release-control posture, fraud and safety escalation rules, commercialization notes, and validated contract completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to consumer electronics and appliance retail execution, order and reservation handling, payment and serial evidence review, customer release control, completion evidence, and pricing, refund, fraud, or safety escalation.

Authority sources cover FTC order and warranty guidance, California refund-policy law, CFPB billing-dispute guidance, NIST price-verification standards, CPSC recall workflow guidance, PHMSA battery and reverse-logistics constraints, and GS1 item-identity standards.

Evaluation scenarios cover incomplete inputs, missing completion evidence, order and payment mismatch, refund-policy exceptions, fraud holds, high-volume intake, terminology fidelity, adjacent-lane rejection, low-confidence escalation, conflicting-policy handling, and damaged-battery stop conditions.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/consumer-electronics-and-appliance-retail-and-service/electronics-retail-specialist/ so electronics retail execution remains separate from pricing, guest-services, fraud, merchandising, and operations lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around price overrides, refund exceptions, fraud adjudication, and recalled or damaged-battery handling.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated consumer-electronics-and-appliance retail execution specialist that supports evidence-backed order progression and policy-aware escalation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
